project: Royal Caribbean DAM — Smart Search & Upload
overview: Three improvements: (1) semantic query expansion — before searching, use Claude Haiku to expand the user's query into related keywords so searches like 'happy family' find images tagged with 'smiling', 'children', 'joy'; (2) smart upload — drop zone accepts any mix of files and folders, recursively extracts all images without asking the user to choose; (3) asset download — a Download button in the detail modal lets content authors download the full-resolution image directly.
goals: Search understands intent, not just exact keywords — 'happy family' returns images of smiling people even if those words aren't in metadata
Upload accepts files, folders, or any mix — no prompt asking user to choose
Recursive folder traversal extracts all image files (jpg, jpeg, png, gif, webp) at any depth
Query expansion is transparent to the user — results just feel smarter
Content authors can download the full-resolution asset from the detail modal with one click
nonGoals: No vector embeddings or semantic similarity DB — expansion runs at query time only
No changes to FTS5 index or DB schema
No UI to show expanded terms to the user
No upload progress changes — existing per-file progress flow unchanged
No server-side folder upload — expansion happens in the browser via webkitdirectory or DataTransferItem API
successMetrics: Searching 'happy family' returns assets tagged with children, smiling, joy, or similar
Searching 'sunset ship' returns assets with tags like golden hour, dusk, aerial, ocean
Dropping a folder onto the upload zone queues all images inside it recursively
Dropping a mix of files and a folder works without error
If Claude is unavailable, search falls back to the original query (no breakage)

stack.ai: Claude Haiku (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) for query expansion — fast and cheap

routes.name: Download Asset
routes.purpose: Streams the full-resolution image file with Content-Disposition: attachment so the browser triggers a file save. Uses the asset's web_image_path or thumbnail_path to locate the file.

uiNotes: Query expansion: no UI changes — happens server-side transparently

Upload drop zone: remove any folder/file toggle or prompt. The drop zone should accept both via the HTML input attribute accept and the DataTransfer API. Use input[type=file] with webkitdirectory disabled but handle DataTransferItem.webkitGetAsEntry() on drop to recursively read folder contents.
Upload drop zone label: change from 'Drop images or a folder here' to 'Drop images or folders here' (plural)
File type filtering: silently skip non-image files during recursive traversal — only queue jpg, jpeg, png, gif, webp
Download button: placed in the detail modal right panel, next to the CDN URL copy button. Label: 'Download'. Navy background, white text, same style as Copy button.
Download button: renders as an anchor tag (<a href='/api/assets/:id/download' download>) so the browser handles the file save natively. No JS required.
Download button: always visible when an asset is loaded — does not require enrichment or CDN URL to be present
rules: Query expansion: only run if req.app.locals.anthropic is set AND q param is non-empty

Query expansion prompt: 'You are helping search a Royal Caribbean cruise marketing image library. Expand this search query into 6-8 related keywords a content author might use to tag such images. Return ONLY a JSON array of lowercase strings, no markdown. Query: "<q>"'
Query expansion: join original query + expanded terms with OR for FTS5: [q, ...expanded].join(' OR ')
Query expansion: if Claude call fails or returns invalid JSON, fall back to original q — never block the search
Smart upload: use DataTransferItem.webkitGetAsEntry() on drop event to detect folders
Smart upload: recursively read FileSystemDirectoryEntry using readEntries() in a loop until exhausted
Smart upload: filter to image MIME types or extensions: image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .gif, .webp
Smart upload: existing input[type=file] click-to-browse should accept both files and folders — add webkitdirectory as a second file input or use a directory picker toggle
Smart upload: the existing per-file upload queue and progress UI is unchanged — just feed it the recursively collected File objects

stories.title: Semantic query expansion via Claude Haiku
stories.description: As a content author, I want to search with natural language like 'happy family on deck' and get relevant results even when those exact words aren't in the metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets route is made async
An expandQuery(anthropic, q) async function is added in routes/assets.js
expandQuery calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 with the RC-specific prompt and returns original q joined with expanded terms using FTS5 OR syntax
If anthropic is null/undefined, expandQuery returns q unchanged
If the Claude call throws or returns invalid JSON, expandQuery catches the error and returns original q unchanged
The expanded query string is passed to searchAssets() in place of the original q
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?q=sunset returns results (expansion is mocked or skipped in tests)
Supertest test: search still works when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set (falls back to plain FTS5)

stories.title: Smart upload — recursive folder and mixed drop support
stories.description: As a content author, I want to drop a folder of images onto the upload zone and have all images inside it queued automatically, without any prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: UploadModal drop zone handles onDrop with DataTransferItem.webkitGetAsEntry() to detect folders vs files
A recursive async function traverseEntry(entry) collects all File objects from FileSystemFileEntry and FileSystemDirectoryEntry at any depth
Only image files are collected: mime type starts with 'image/' OR extension is jpg/jpeg/png/gif/webp (case-insensitive)
Non-image files are silently skipped — no error shown
Dropping a mix of image files and a folder containing images queues all images from both sources
The collected File array is passed to the existing upload queue logic unchanged
Drop zone label updated to 'Drop images or folders here'
Click-to-browse input: add a second input[type=file][webkitdirectory][multiple] button labelled 'Browse folder' alongside the existing 'Browse files' button, both feeding the same queue
Vitest test: traverseEntry correctly filters out non-image files
Vitest test: drop with a mix of file and folder entries queues only image files

stories.title: Asset download from detail modal
stories.description: As a content author, I want to download the full-resolution asset directly from the detail modal so I can use it in my campaign without navigating elsewhere.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets/:id/download added to server/src/routes/assets.js
Endpoint resolves the asset's web_image_path or thumbnail_path relative to DATA_DIR (same logic as the /media static route)
Endpoint sets Content-Disposition: attachment; filename='<original filename>' and streams the file
Returns 404 { error: 'Asset not found' } if asset ID does not exist
Returns 404 { error: 'File not found' } if the image file is missing on disk
AssetDetailModal right panel shows a Download button rendered as <a href='/api/assets/:id/download' download> with navy styling matching the Copy button
Download button is always shown when asset is loaded (not conditional on CDN URL or enrichment)
client/src/api/assets.js exports getAssetDownloadUrl(id) that returns the download URL string
Supertest test: GET /api/assets/:id/download for a valid asset returns 200 with Content-Disposition header
Supertest test: GET /api/assets/nonexistent/download returns 404
